data.title: Kimberly-Clark looks to Vietnam amid the global baby birth rate slump
data.description:
Kimberly-Clark Corporation (KMB) designated Vietnam as a key manufacturing hub for its Huggies diapers and other personal care products in Southeast Asia. The decision reflects both the nation's growing consumer market and its importance in the household product company’s global supply chain realignment. 

The company is concentrating production in Vietnam to serve not only the local market but also neighboring countries, leveraging lower costs, improving infrastructure, and favorable trade access in ASEAN. Notably, Vietnam's baby diaper market is expanding steadily, supported by rising incomes, urbanization, and increased hygiene awareness, with recent research projecting mid- to high-single-digit annual volume growth over the coming years.

An important factor is that Kimberly-Clark (KMB) has acknowledged that the global baby boom is over and seeks growth in emerging Asian markets, where it is trying to close the gap with market leader Unicharm (OTCPK:UNICY) and Procter & Gamble (PG). Using Vietnam as a factory hub signals a long-term bet on Southeast Asia’s consumer growth even as birth rates slow, with the strategy hinging on scale, cost efficiency, and the ability to out-innovate rivals in a crowded diaper landscape.

data.title: Wall Street rises on fresh economic signals ahead of Thanksgiving
data.description:
Wall Street watched stocks climb higher on Wednesday, with traders and investors digesting new economic data amid ongoing rate-cut speculation.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) which rallied north of 650 points on Tuesday was last higher by 0.3%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) pushed up by 0.3% and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) advanced by 0.3%. 

Looking at the S&P 500 by sector, nine of the 11 segments are trending higher, with Utilities leading the gains. On the downside, Communication Services has been the weakest performing area so far. 

Treasury yields advanced as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved higher by 3 basis points to 4.03% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.49%. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims surprised lower in the week ended November 22. Durable goods orders slowed in September, as expected. 

“Stocks are celebrating the likelihood of a December rate cut, as it's becoming clear that the labor market is the priority for the Federal Reserve, and another rate cut would help to protect against any further cooling of the job market,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated. 

“It's becoming more clear that the stock market declines seen over most of November were simply a pullback after an extremely strong run in October and September,” Stanley added. 

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of Robinhood Markets (HOOD) tracked higher by 6.2% on the day while shares of Zscaler (ZS) dropped 11.5%.

For reference, markets will be closed on November 27, Thursday, for the Thanksgiving holiday.

data.title: Plymouth Industrial REIT falls after Sixth Street abandons earlier proposal
data.description:
Plymouth Industrial REIT (PLYM) dropped 2.4% after Sixth Street Partners said it's abandoning its previously announced non-binding proposal to acquire the company.

Sixth Street said it no longer intends to engage in discussions or negotiations regarding its potential acquisition of Plymouth Industrial REIT (PLYM), the investor said in a  13D/A filing on Wednesday. 

The disclosure comes after Plymouth Industrial (PLYM) last month agreed to be sold to Makarora Management, along with Ares Alternative Credit funds, in an all-cash deal valued at about $2.1 billion, or $22 a share. 

Sixth Street Partners originally made a non-binding proposal to buy Plymouth Industrial (PLYM) for $24.10 a share in August.

data.title: Chicago PMI unexpectedly falls deeper into contraction in November
data.description: The Chicago PMI dropped to 36.3 in November from 43.8 in the prior month, compared with the 44.3 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Wednesday.

data.title: Patria Investments to buy 51% stake in Brazilian investment manager Solis
data.description:
Patria Investments (PAX) on Wednesday announced an agreement to acquire 51% of the Brazilian investment manager Solis Investimentos.

The Cayman Islands-based alternative asset manager did not disclose financial terms of the transaction.

The addition is expected to increase Patria's total credit fee-earning AUM by over 40% to more than $11.7 pro forma as of Q3 2025.

Solis's team is set to remain in their current roles, with offices in Fortaleza and São Paulo, Brazil.

data.title: Venture Global says Shell waging campaign to damage LNG business
data.description:
Venture Global (VG) has accused Shell (SHEL) of waging a "three-year campaign" to damage its liquefied natural gas business after Shell appealed an arbitration loss, the Financial Times reported Wednesday.

In an email to employees obtained by FT, Venture Global (VG) co-founders Michael Sabel and Robert Pender blasted Shell's (SHEL) "misguided decision" to appeal the arbitration loss, saying the action "relies on completely baseless claims and is an unfortunate continuation of their three-year campaign to damage Venture Global. This behavior should be very concerning to Shell's employees, board of directors and shareholders."

Venture Global (VG) defeated Shell's (SHEL) arbitration claim at the International Chamber of Commerce, but in October it lost a separate case against BP (BP), which is seeking more than $1 billion in damages; earlier this month, Shell challenged its arbitration defeat in a New York state court in an attempt to overturn the panel’s finding.

Shell (SHEL), BP (BP) and other European companies alleged Venture Global (VG) breached supply contracts by failing to deliver shipments under long-term supply contracts, instead selling them on the spot market to profit from higher prices triggered by Russia’s 2022 invasion of
Ukraine.

Venture Global's (VG) legal difficulties have not limited its ability to sign new supply deals, with the company announcing late Tuesday that Tokyo Gas agreed to buy 1 million tons/year of LNG over 20 years, starting from 2030 - the most recent of several supply deals.

data.title: Société Générale forecasts above-trend growth and a 7,300 S&P 500 target for 2026
data.description:
Société Générale is projecting a wider-than-usual range of potential outcomes for the S&P 500 (SP500) in 2026, with the Federal Reserve’s policy path expected to play an outsized role in shaping market direction.

In a recent investor note, the firm said the benchmark index could reach 7,300 during the first half of the year, a level it also expects the S&P 500 to maintain through year-end. That forecast is based on a valuation of roughly 22 times projected 2027 earnings of $333 per share, implying an upside of about 11% from current levels. 

The investment bank outlined several alternative scenarios as well. Its bear-case view places the index near 6,700, while its bull-case target sits at 7,700, reflecting the unusually wide dispersion of possible outcomes tied to monetary policy uncertainty. 

Société Générale anticipates two additional rate cuts before Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s term concludes in May. The firm also suggested that a forthcoming leadership transition at the central bank could bolster investor sentiment. 

Looking beyond monetary policy, the bank expects 2026 to deliver above-trend economic growth. A combination of strong fiscal support and an accelerating capital expenditure cycle—spanning both technology and traditional industries—is seen as a key pillar of the improving outlook. 

S&P 500 Funds: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Autodesk raises full-year guidance; prompts analysts to increase estimates
data.description:
Autodesk (ADSK) easily surpassed estimates in its third-quarter results and increased its full-year guidance, prompting analysts to increase their own forward-looking estimates and expect possible multiple expansion.

Shares had jumped 7% during early trading on Wednesday.

"Multiple data points build confidence in the margin expansion story, including outperformance in Q3 driven by cost discipline and operating leverage, stronger-than-expected mix to the Autodesk store, and sales and marketing optimization efficiencies benefiting the Q4 outlook," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Elizabeth Porter, in a Wednesday investor note.

Morgan Stanley maintained its Overweight rating and $385 price target on Autodesk. It also increased its full-year EPS estimate to $10.20 from $9.89.

"While FY26 growth has been steady between 11-12%, management expressed prudence will be provided with the formal FY27 outlook on the Q4 call," Porter added. "Specifically, the outlook will incorporate the potential for (1) execution risk following actions to optimize Sales and Marketing, and (2) macro uncertainty in the ~12 month-forward period."

Meanwhile, KeyBanc also reiterated its Overweight rating and maintained its $365 price target. The financial firm increased its fourth quarter fiscal 2026 revenue estimate to $1.9B from $1.85B and its full-year estimate to $7.15B from $7.05B. It increased its full-year fiscal 2027 revenue estimate to $8.05B from $7.86B.

"Notably, our out-year revenue growth assumptions are moving higher to reflect the Company's continued underlying business momentum," said KeyBanc analysts, led by Jason Celino, in an investor note. "We are encouraged by Autodesk's better results and consistent execution, giving us greater confidence in our thesis that Autodesk could see continued multiple expansion toward broader industrial SW peers."

Autodesk CFO Janesh Moorjani said during the earnings call he expects margins to continue to improve in the quarters ahead despite headwinds.

"Third quarter GAAP and non-GAAP operating margins were 25% and 38%, respectively, reflecting year-over-year increases of approximately 330 and 120 basis points, respectively," Moorjani said. "This reflected operating leverage and ongoing cost discipline and was partly offset by the margin drag from the new transaction model. Our margin progress this year sets us up well to achieve the long-term margin goals we talked about at our Investor Day. We still expect progress towards that goal to be nonlinear, given incremental headwinds to reported margins in fiscal '27 from the new transaction model."

He also said Autodesk plans to buy back about $1.3B in stocks this year, which is a 50% increase compared to fiscal 2025.

Autodesk competitors PTC (PTC) and Dassault Systèmes SE (OTCPK:DASTY) were showing mixed action. PTC was flat, and Dassault Systèmes had increased 1.8%.

data.title: MainStreet Bancshares announces CFO retirement
data.description:
MainStreet Bancshares (MNSB) on Wednesday said that CFO Thomas Chmelik will retire from the post on December 31, 2025.

Chmelik is set to continue to serve as senior executive vice president and secretary of MainStreet Bank and the financial holding company through December 31, 2027.

data.title: Correlation no more: Nasdaq’s comeback leaves bitcoin in the dust
data.description:
Apollo’s chief economist, Torsten Slok, is drawing attention to a growing divergence between bitcoin (BTC-USD) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND), two assets that have historically moved in close alignment.

While both are typically regarded as risk-on plays and often respond similarly to shifts in investor sentiment, that relationship has fractured in recent weeks, Slok noted.

The Nasdaq Composite has been steadily clawing back ground, narrowing its distance from record levels following the earlier month’s market turbulence. The tech-heavy benchmark now sits roughly 4% below its all-time high, reflecting renewed confidence in the sector and a broader appetite for growth-oriented equities. 

Bitcoin, however, has taken a markedly different path. The cryptocurrency has suffered a much steeper pullback, sliding more than 31% from its peak as traders reassess digital-asset valuations amid heightened volatility.

The widening gap between equity markets and bitcoin performance has raised questions about whether crypto is breaking away from traditional risk-asset behavior or simply undergoing a deeper cyclical correction.

Slok’s chart, referenced below, illustrates the notable breakdown in the once-strong correlation—an emerging trend that analysts are watching closely as markets attempt to recalibrate heading into year-end.

Nasdaq ETFs: (QQQ), (QQQM), (SQQQ), (TQQQ), (QLD), and  (QID).

Bitcoin ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).
